headings.usage: Hero typography, Car Card Titles, Main Headers - gives a heavy-weight, authoritative automotive feel.

body.usage: General body text, descriptions, standard UI elements.

mono.usage: Countdown timers, launch dates, metrics, telemetry data. Adds a technical 'timing board' feel.

spacing_and_layout.general: Dense, technical layout. Use generous padding inside containers but maintain sharp, un-rounded geometric edges (rounded-none).
spacing_and_layout.grid: Control Room Grid for the directory: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4. Gap: gap-6. Grid items must stretch to fill height.
spacing_and_layout.cards: Sharp 1px solid borders (border-white/10), flat background (#111111), no drop shadows. Inside padding p-5 or p-6.

header.style: Sticky top (z-50), Solid Background (#050505) with a hard bottom border (1px border-white/10). No blur, pure contrast.
header.elements: Contains Brand Logo (Left) and the Category Segmented Control (Center/Right).

segmented_control.usage: Category toggle between 'Performance / JDM' and 'Everyday / Euro Classic'.

car_card.layout: Vertical flex layout (flex-col). Height: h-full. Top: Image slot (h-48 object-cover). Middle: Car_Name (Barlow Condensed text-2xl uppercase), Category (Chivo text-xs text-white/50), Launch_Date. Bottom: Status Badge + Countdown.
car_card.interactions: Hover state: border-white/30, slight background lighten (bg-[#1A1A1A]), image scale slightly.

status_badge.eligible: bg-[#00FF66]/10 text-[#00FF66] border border-[#00FF66]/30 px-2 py-1 text-xs font-bold uppercase rounded-none w-fit inline-flex items-center gap-1
status_badge.active: bg-[#FF5500]/10 text-[#FF5500] border border-[#FF5500]/30 px-2 py-1 text-xs font-bold uppercase rounded-none w-fit inline-flex items-center gap-1

countdown_display.layout: Positioned below the active badge, prominent timing board style.

motion.hover: Mechanical, instant or very fast transitions (duration-150 ease-out).
motion.entrance: Staggered fade-up for directory grid items to feel like a dashboard booting up.

hero_background.description: Checkered fabric texture for very subtle background opacity (e.g., opacity-5) in hero/header section.

placeholder_jdm_2.description: Black sports car in dark room.

placeholder_euro_2.description: Green sports car parked in the dark.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build the directory application using React and Tailwind CSS.
Mock a primary database 'VRT_Registry' with approx 12-20 items for demonstration, containing: ID, Car_Name, Category, Launch_Date, VRT_Freedom_Date, External_Link.
Implement a mobile-first responsive grid for the car cards (grid-cols-1 on mobile, up to grid-cols-4 on XL screens).

In the sticky nav header, implement the category toggle ('Performance / JDM' vs 'Everyday / Euro Classic') as a custom segmented control. DO NOT use a standard dropdown. It should visually look like a toggle switch/tab bar with sharp edges.
If Time_Left <= 0: Show the green badge '👑 €200 FLAT VRT ELIGIBLE' and HIDE the countdown.
If Time_Left > 0: Show the amber badge '⏳ COUNTDOWN ACTIVE'. Below it, format countdown: If >30 days, display 'X Months, Y Days Remaining'. If <= 30 days, display 'X Days Remaining' in bold.
Typography: Import and apply 'Barlow Condensed' for headings/car names, 'IBM Plex Mono' for countdowns/metrics, and 'Chivo' for body text. All fonts are available on Google Fonts.

Aesthetic: Maintain a stark, high-contrast 'Motorsport Editorial' look. Use deep black backgrounds (#050505), sharp borders (rounded-none), and high contrast white text. Avoid all gradients, drop shadows, and soft UI elements.
Ensure all interactive elements (buttons, segmented controls, car links) have 'data-testid' attributes (e.g., data-testid='category-toggle-jdm').
